skill.name: allra-api-design
skill.description: Spring Boot REST API design rules for Allra backend services, including package structure, DTO naming, validation, and response conventions.
skill.summary: Guide Spring Boot API design with Allra backend conventions.
skill.author: Allra-Fintech

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns in SKILL.md. Manual review found these are false positives from Markdown code fences, inline examples, and descriptive text. No executable scripts, network access, filesystem access, prompt injection, or malicious intent were found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backtick Examples
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. The external command detections are Markdown code fences and inline formatting for package trees, DTO names, Java examples, JSON examples, and checklist text. They are documentation examples, not shell execution instructions or runnable skill scripts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are inside Markdown examples or inline backtick formatting. No command interpreter, subprocess call, or instruction to execute external commands appears in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Match
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. The weak cryptography detections at the front matter are text matches in the skill name and description. They do not define or recommend DES, MD5, SHA1, or any other weak algorithm.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of lines 2 and 3 found only the skill name and natural language description. There is no cryptographic implementation or guidance in this file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: REST Controller Examples
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. The system reconnaissance detections are Spring controller examples using request bodies and path variables. They describe API design conventions and do not collect host, user, network, or environment information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The listed lines are method signatures in illustrative Java API snippets. No system inspection commands, environment reads, or reconnaissance behavior are present.

content.value_statement: Backend teams need consistent API structure, DTO names, validation, and response patterns. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to follow Allra Spring Boot conventions.
actual_capabilities: Recommends domain-based package structure for Java backend features.
Defines Request, Response, and internal DTO naming conventions.
Encourages Java record usage for simple DTO classes.
Shows Spring REST controller patterns for common CRUD endpoints.
Describes Bean Validation expectations for request DTOs.
Provides a checklist for new API endpoint work.
limitations: It documents conventions but does not generate complete production services by itself.
It is tailored to Allra backend standards and may need adaptation for other teams.
It does not include authentication, authorization, or database transaction policy details.
It does not validate live code or run automated tests.

use_cases.title: Create a New Domain API
use_cases.description: Plan package folders, controller endpoints, request DTOs, response DTOs, and service boundaries for a new Spring domain.

use_cases.title: Standardize DTO Naming
use_cases.description: Review request, response, internal transfer, query result, and event DTO names against Allra naming expectations.

use_cases.title: Prepare API Implementation Tasks
use_cases.description: Turn a feature request into an implementation checklist aligned with Allra REST and validation conventions.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Simple API
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a new endpoint structure.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Allra API design rules to plan a new Spring Boot API for [domain]. Include package structure, endpoint names, and DTO names.

prompt_templates.title: Review DTO Names
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these DTO names for Allra conventions: [names]. Identify which should be Request, Response, or internal Dto types.

prompt_templates.title: Design Validation Rules
prompt_templates.scenario: Add request validation to an API design.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design request DTO validation for [operation]. Follow the Allra guidance for Spring Bean Validation and explain each required field.

prompt_templates.title: Audit an API Design
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of a complete feature design.
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this Spring API design against Allra package, DTO, controller, response, and checklist standards. Provide concrete corrections: [design].

output_examples.output: The response would outline a product domain package, a create request, a product response, validation needs, and a controller route for product creation.
The response would classify the event object as an internal DTO and the signup result as a client response object.

output_examples.input: Check whether a user update API follows the checklist.
output_examples.output: The response would compare the design against package structure, DTO naming, record usage, validation, REST naming, and internal DTO suffix rules.

best_practices: Start from the domain package structure before naming classes.
Keep client DTOs and internal DTOs separate by naming and package location.
Apply validation to every request DTO that receives client input.
anti_patterns: Mixing controller, entity, repository, and service code in one package.
Using internal DTOs as public API response contracts.
Creating request DTOs without validation for required client fields.

faq.question: What framework is this skill designed for?
faq.answer: It is designed for Java 17 or later with Spring Boot 3.2 or later.

faq.question: Does this skill create runnable application code?
faq.answer: It provides conventions and examples. The assistant can use them when creating code, but the skill itself is documentation.

faq.question: Can non-Allra teams use it?
faq.answer: Yes, but teams should adapt naming, response formats, and package rules to their own standards.

faq.question: Does it include database schema design?
faq.answer: No. It mentions repositories and entities, but it does not define database schema rules.

faq.question: Does it cover API versioning?
faq.answer: It shows versioned paths as an example and says versioning depends on project policy.

faq.question: Which assistants can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
